original_abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) pretrained on massive source code repositories have significantly advanced the field of code intelligence. However, current models face two primary obstacles. First, they typically rely on rigid architectures—either encoder-only, decoder-only, or a fixed encoder-decoder structure—which prevents them from adapting to the optimal configuration for a specific downstream task. Second, they often utilize a narrow set of pretraining objectives that may not align with the requirements of diverse tasks, leading to suboptimal performance.

To overcome these challenges, we introduce CodeT5+, a family of encoder-decoder LLMs for code designed for modular flexibility. CodeT5+ allows for the dynamic combination of component modules to suit a wide array of code-related applications. This versatility is supported by a diverse mixture of pretraining objectives, including span denoising, contrastive learning, text-code matching, and causal language modeling, trained on both unimodal and bimodal multilingual corpora. Furthermore, we implement a compute-efficient scaling strategy by initializing CodeT5+ with frozen, off-the-shelf LLMs, avoiding the need for training from scratch, and explore instruction-tuning to improve alignment with natural language prompts.

We evaluated CodeT5+ across more than 20 code-related benchmarks in zero-shot, finetuning, and instruction-tuning settings. Our models achieve state-of-the-art (SoTA) results on numerous tasks. Notably, our instruction-tuned CodeT5+ 16B sets a new benchmark on the HumanEval code generation task with 35.0% pass@1 and 54.5% pass@10, outperforming other open-source code LLMs and even surpassing OpenAI's code-cushman-001.
humanized_abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) pretrained on large source code repositories have revolutionized the field of code intelligence. However, current models suffer from two major drawbacks. First, most LLMs have to be stuck to a fixed architecture, either being an encoder, a decoder, or a mixture of both, which is not optimal for every single downstream task. Second, they are pretrained on a fixed set of objectives, which might not be suitable for all tasks, which leads to potential suboptimal performance.

Recent advancements in LLMs have enabled improved analysis and generation of natural language. However, current LLMs are limited when it comes to coding. For example, they often have to re-explain basics and lack precise programming knowledge, making coding hard to accomplish. To address these challenges, we propose a novel family of encoder-decoder LLMs for code named CodeT5+, in which multiple coding modules can be combined in a flexible, modular manner, to support a large number of coding applications. The diverse pretraining objectives that include span denoising, contrastive learning, text-code matching, and causal language modeling are pre-trained on a large, diverse unimodal and bimodal, multilingual dataset. We propose a computation efficient way to scale up CodeT5+ by initializing and using frozen off-the-shelf LLMs and seek to fine-tune them via instruction-tuning in order to be more suitable for specific language instructions provided for coding tasks.

We evaluated CodeT5+ on over 20 code-related benchmarks in zero-shot, finetuning and instruction-tuning settings, and obtain SoTA results on a majority of them. Notably, our instruction-tuned CodeT5+ 16B achieves SoTA performance on the HumanEval code generation task with 35.0% pass@1 and 54.5% pass@10, outperforming other open-source code LLMs and even leading the recently released OpenAI code-cushman-001.

document.input:
Large language models (LLMs) pretrained on massive source code repositories have significantly advanced the field of code intelligence. However, current models face two primary obstacles. First, they typically rely on rigid architectures—either encoder-only, decoder-only, or a fixed encoder-decoder structure—which prevents them from adapting to the optimal configuration for a specific downstream task. To overcome these challenges, we introduce CodeT5+, a family of encoder-decoder LLMs for code designed for modular flexibility. This versatility is supported by a diverse mixture of pretraining objectives, including span denoising, contrastive learning, text-code matching, and causal language modeling, trained on both unimodal and bimodal multilingual corpora. Furthermore, we implement a compute-efficient scaling strategy by initializing CodeT5+ with frozen, off-the-shelf LLMs, avoiding the need for training from scratch, and explore instruction-tuning to improve alignment with natural language prompts.

We evaluated CodeT5+ across more than 20 code-related benchmarks in zero-shot, finetuning, and instruction-tuning settings. Our models achieve state-of-the-art (SoTA) results on numerous tasks. Notably, our instruction-tuned CodeT5+ 16B sets a new benchmark on the HumanEval code generation task with 35.0% pass@1 and 54.5% pass@10, outperforming other open-source code LLMs and even surpassing OpenAI's code-cushman-001.
